title: documentation update: Identify required tools
prompt: Review the configuration below. During documentation update, the agent can call every administrative tool even though the task needs only read, inspect, test, and limited change operations, creating an excessive tool surface. How should the team identify required tools in this situation?
artifact:
{
  "tool": "one documentation repository",
  "actions": [
    "read",
    "write",
    "delete",
    "admin"
  ],
  "repositories": [
    "*"
  ],
  "environments": [
    "*"
  ],
  "expires_at": null,
  "observed_issue": "the agent can call every administrative tool even though the task needs only read, inspect, test, and limited change operations"
}
options: Run documentation update in an isolated, repository- and environment-scoped context with no path to unrelated production resources.
For documentation update, verify durable state, use idempotency and bounded retries, preserve rollback evidence, and escalate when needed.
For documentation update, keep organization-admin permissions for documentation agent, but add detailed logs after each tool call to monitor misuse.
Expose only the read, inspect, test, and change tools required for documentation update; remove unrelated administrative capabilities.
explanation: This response addresses an excessive tool surface at the point where it occurs. Tool selection should follow the task, not the maximum capability available. Minimize the callable surface before configuring permissions.

wrong_answer_explanations.A: That control supports evaluate execution context and scope, but it leaves an excessive tool surface unresolved.
wrong_answer_explanations.B: This would help the team implement robust error handling and recovery, not correct an excessive tool surface.
wrong_answer_explanations.C: Logging records activity but does not reduce authority or blast radius.

notes_for_reviewer: Verify identify required tools terminology, artifact realism, and one-best-answer defensibility against the current public GH-600 study guide before publication.
